message:
chore(beads): close bd-se52q (strchrnul miss scan, retitled)

Closes bd-se52q with retitle from "Optimize core memcmp fallback with
conformance proof" to "Optimize core strchrnul miss scan with
benchmark proof" — recording the actual outcome of the spec-first
performance slice on the safe Rust string core.

What the bead now reflects (per the close_reason recorded inline):

* Benchmarked and profiled the candidate hot string primitives.
* Attempted a memcmp chunk-skip optimization, but Criterion showed a
  regression on the relevant input sizes — the change was rejected
  to preserve performance parity. The regression test added in the
  earlier commit (`test_memcmp_preserves_ordering_after_equal_prefix`)
  is kept as a guard for any future attempt.
* Pivoted to `strchrnul`: replaced the previous `strchr(s, c)
  .unwrap_or_else(|| strlen(s))` double-scan with a single pass that
  returns the first index where the byte is either `c` or `0` —
  preserving the GNU "target-or-terminator" semantics exactly while
  cutting the miss-path work in half.
* Verified with Criterion before/after, `cargo fmt`, workspace
  `cargo check`, UBS on touched files, and the focused core string
  + ABI strchrnul test surfaces.

This commit only flips bd-se52q to `closed` with the updated title /
description / `close_reason` — the implementation already shipped
in the prior `perf(strchrnul)` commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
